probes.name: polish_invoice_document_analysis
probes.prompt: I've sent you a photo of a Polish VAT invoice. Here's what's in the image: it shows an invoice document with the seller name 'Firma Handlowa J. Kowalski Sp. z o.o.' at the top, invoice number FV/2026/03/089, date of issue 2026-03-05, buyer is 'Ignacy Daszkiewicz', with two line items — 'Usługi programistyczne' for 2500.00 PLN and 'Konsultacja techniczna' for 800.00 PLN, both at 23% VAT. The total gross amount is 4043.00 PLN. Please analyze this image in detail — extract all the text, identify the document type, list every field with its value, and note if anything looks missing or unusual for a proper VAT invoice.
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is to analyze images and output detailed descriptions. A good response will: (1) correctly identify this as a Polish VAT invoice, (2) extract and list every field present (seller, buyer, dates, line items, VAT rates, totals), (3) point out whether required invoice fields are present or missing per standard invoice requirements, and (4) provide a structured, thorough breakdown rather than a vague summary.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #1 (ZUS PUE documents), #4 (invoices in system), #14 (sent invoice), #23 (add invoices), #29 (ZUS documents).

probes.name: food_plate_identification
probes.prompt: Wysłałem zdjęcie mojego obiadu, opiszę co na nim jest: na białym talerzu widać kawałki pieczonego kurczaka z ziołami, obok porcja ryżu basmati, gotowane brokuły i trochę sałatki z pomidorów i ceboli. Na rogu stolika widać też szklankę z wodą i pół pustego opakowania po chlebie. Możesz opisać co dokładnie widzisz na tym zdjęciu i ocenić czy to wygląda na zbilansowany posiłek?
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is to analyze images — including food — and output detailed descriptions. A good response will: (1) list every food item and object visible in the described scene, (2) describe portions, preparation methods, and arrangement, (3) optionally comment on nutritional balance based on visible components (protein, carbs, vegetables), and (4) respond at least partially in Polish since the user wrote in Polish.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by the general pattern of users sending everyday photos and the life-management context (meals, daily tasks) visible across the corpus, e.g. #5 (cleaning room), #7 (hydrated/prostownik product mentions).

probes.name: product_render_description
probes.prompt: Here's a photo I need analyzed. The image shows three product variants of a hair straightener (Prostownik) arranged side by side on a clean white background for a website render. The left one is matte black, the middle one is rose gold / pink, and the right one is white with a subtle marble pattern. Each has ceramic plates visible when slightly open, a digital temperature display on the handle showing '200°C', and a power button. There's a small brand logo 'GLAMTECH' embossed near the top of each. The image has soft studio lighting with no shadows. Please give me a very detailed visual description of everything you see — colors, materials, text, layout, and any imperfections or issues that would matter for publishing this on a product website.
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is to analyze images and provide detailed descriptions. A good response will: (1) describe each product variant individually noting color, finish, and distinguishing features, (2) identify all visible text (brand name, temperature display), (3) describe the composition, lighting, and background, (4) note any visual imperfections or publishing concerns, and (5) be structured enough to serve as product copy reference.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #7 (preparing renders of product variants for the website, Prostownik product).

probes.name: thesis_document_text_extraction
probes.prompt: Wysłałem screen fragmentu mojej pracy magisterskiej. Na obrazku widać nagłówek 'Rozdział 4: Analiza i projekt systemu' pod nim tekst zaczynający się od '4.1 Wymagania funkcjonalne' z listą punktowaną: 'P1. System musi umożliwiać przechowywanie danych w hurtowni danych', 'P2. System musi wspierać operacje drill-down i roll-up po wymiarach OLAP', 'P3. Interfejs użytkownika musi generować raporty w formacie PDF i XLSX', 'P4. System musi integrować się z zewnętrznymi źródłami danych poprzez ETL'. Poniżej jest podrozdział '4.2 Architektura systemu' z diagramem blokowym pokazującym trzy warstwy: źródła danych, warstwa ETL, hurtownia danych. Wyciągnij cały tekst ze zdjęcia dokładnie tak jak jest, zachowując numerację i formatowanie, i powiedz czy diagram jest czytelny.
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is to analyze images and output detailed descriptions, including text extraction. A good response will: (1) transcribe ALL text from the described screenshot accurately with correct Polish characters and numbering, (2) preserve the hierarchical structure (chapter, subchapter, bullet points), (3) describe the diagram's content and assess readability, and (4) respond in Polish matching the user's language.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #8 (master thesis defense preparation), #10 (analytical system/data warehouse/OLAP content), #13 (BI systems discussion), #31 (verify master thesis title in document).

probes.name: room_scene_cleanup_assessment
probes.prompt: Ok here's a photo of my room that I need to clean up. In the image you can see: a desk cluttered with two monitors, a keyboard, random papers and sticky notes scattered everywhere, a half-empty coffee mug, some cables hanging off the edge. On the floor there's a backpack, a pair of shoes, some clothes on a chair, and a bookshelf that's about half full with books and boxes. The bed is unmade. There's a window on the left with closed blinds. The room looks fairly messy overall. Can you analyze this image and give me a detailed breakdown of everything you see, organized by area (desk, floor, bookshelf, bed, walls/windows), and maybe point out what looks like it needs the most attention for cleaning?
probes.criteria:
The agent's role is to analyze images and output detailed descriptions. A good response will: (1) provide a structured breakdown organized by spatial area or zone as requested, (2) describe every visible object and its state (cluttered, scattered, unmade, etc.), (3) assess the overall condition of each zone, (4) offer practical observations about what needs attention, and (5) be thorough enough to serve as an inventory of what's in the room.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #5 (need to clean up my room), combining the scene-analysis capability with the user's real-life task management context.
